site.summary: Built from official county records and regional news coverage so the homepage stays focused on the most significant local political events.

featured.title: March Primary Results Prompt Ongoing Review of Election Records
featured.summary: Official March 3 primary results remain the baseline reference for Davidson County voters. Late-March reporting from Carolina Public Press and NC Newsline showed that statewide canvass and recount procedures continued after election night in a few closely watched contests.
featured.keywords: march 2026 primary results canvass recount ncsbe davidson county election record official results portal
featured.imageAlt: North Carolina flag representing statewide election coverage

featured.title: County Boards Continue the March Meeting Cycle
featured.summary: The Davidson County Agenda Center lists recent commissioners, planning board, and board of health meetings, including March 2026 entries and links to agendas, minutes, and, where available, videos.
featured.keywords: commissioners agenda center march 2026 davidson county meeting agenda planning board board of health video minutes

featured.title: Board of Health Sets April Review of Priorities and Budget Updates
featured.summary: Davidson Local reported that the county Board of Health planned an April 14 meeting focused on community health priorities, budget updates, agenda approval, and public comment.
featured.keywords: board of health april 14 priorities budget davidson local public comment community health
featured.imageAlt: Board of Health story image from Davidson Local

articles.title: Election Canvass Keeps Attention on the Official Record
articles.summary: Official March 3 results remain the key public record for Davidson County and surrounding races. Statewide reporting later in March highlighted how certification, recounts, and protests can continue after election night when margins stay narrow.
articles.keywords: election canvass results ncsbe recount official record
articles.imageAlt: North Carolina flag representing election record coverage

articles.title: March and April Agendas Show How County Business Moves Forward
articles.summary: The official Davidson County Agenda Center continues to post meeting schedules for the commissioners, planning board, and board of health. Those public records provide the most reliable picture of what is being discussed, delayed, or moved forward.
articles.keywords: agenda center commissioners planning board board of health meetings public records

articles.title: Board of Health Agenda Highlights Priorities, Budget, and Public Comment
articles.summary: Davidson Local reported that the April 14 Board of Health meeting was expected to include welcome remarks, agenda approval, public comment, and updates on county health priorities and budget planning.
articles.keywords: board of health priorities budget public comment april 14
articles.imageAlt: Board of Health story image from Davidson Local

articles.title: Candidate Filing and Election Notices Remain Central Before Local Decisions
articles.summary: Before any campaign claims are amplified, the most dependable checkpoints remain the county Board of Elections and the North Carolina State Board of Elections filing guidance. Those records help keep local coverage factual and organized.
articles.keywords: candidate filing local boards official election notices rules deadlines
articles.imageAlt: North Carolina flag representing state filing guidance

articles.title: Public Notice Tools Make Ongoing Updates Easier to Monitor
articles.summary: The Agenda Center includes RSS and notification options, making it easier to review new county postings on a regular 2-to-4-hour cycle when fresh notices or agenda changes appear.
articles.keywords: rss notify me updates every 4 hours agenda center notices
articles.imageAlt: Official Davidson County video thumbnail used for public notice tracking

articles.title: County Race Tracking Benefits from Side-by-Side State and Local Result Checks
articles.summary: Using the state results portal alongside county-level notices helps readers verify whether updates are election-night totals or later administrative changes tied to canvass steps.
articles.keywords: results monitoring county race tracking state local checks ncsbe board of elections
articles.imageAlt: North Carolina flag representing statewide and local election result checks

articles.title: Agenda Posting Patterns Offer Early Signals on Upcoming County Decision Points
articles.summary: Agenda Center timelines can indicate when major topics are moving toward discussion, especially when paired with posted minutes and available meeting video archives.
articles.keywords: agenda posting patterns county decisions minutes video archives commissioners planning board

articles.title: Filing Guidance and County Notices Work Best as a Combined Pre-Election Checklist
articles.summary: State filing guidance explains formal requirements, while county election notices provide local implementation context. Reviewing both reduces confusion before key election milestones.
articles.keywords: filing guidance county notices pre election checklist requirements milestones
articles.imageAlt: North Carolina flag representing filing guidance and election notices

articles.title: Board of Elections Directory Updates Clarify Contact and Filing Access
articles.summary: Recent directory and contact-page checks help residents confirm where to find official notices, filing guidance, and election administration contacts before deadlines approach.
articles.keywords: board of elections directory contacts filing access davidson county

articles.title: Candidate Filing Calendar Checks Help Voters Track Who Has Officially Entered
articles.summary: Monitoring county filing pages and state guidance together can help residents distinguish campaign announcements from officially filed candidacies.
articles.keywords: candidate filing calendar official entries board of elections ncsbe
